# PRD-101: Plugin Contract

> Epic: [Modular Module Runtime](../../epics/10-modular-module-runtime.md)
> Status: Not started

## Overview

POPS is a personal OS: a shell that hosts sandboxed apps and overlays which opt into the platform via a single, typed contract. This PRD defines that contract â€” the `ModuleManifest` an app exports â€” and the build-time aggregator (`@pops/module-registry`) that turns the set of installed manifests into a typed registry the rest of the platform consumes.

Every cross-cutting concern (routing, settings, feature toggles, search, AI/MCP tool surface, URI resolution, migrations, overlay mounting) reads from the registry. There is no second registry, no side-effect import that registers anything, no hand-edited central list.

The contract is the only place a module describes itself. Adding a new app means writing one manifest file and one package; the platform discovers everything else from there.

## Positioning

PRD-098 introduced `ModuleManifest` as a metadata-only type â€” every shipped module exports one, but nothing aggregates them. PRD-100 shipped the env loader (`POPS_APPS` / `POPS_OVERLAYS`) and the `core.shell.manifest` endpoint. Today four parallel registries (settings, features, search, overlay) coexist with the manifest type but ignore it.

PRD-101 completes the picture: it extends the manifest with the missing slots, adds a build-time aggregator, and migrates each existing registry to consume the aggregator. The four current registries collapse into one. PRDs 097â€“100 remain the foundation; PRD-101 makes them load-bearing.

## The Contract

`ModuleManifest` (in `packages/types/src/module-manifest.ts`) is the single artifact a module exports. It carries every slot the platform needs to assemble itself.

| Slot           | Type                         | Purpose                                                                                              |
| -------------- | ---------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `id`           | `string`                     | Canonical id; matches `POPS_APPS` / `POPS_OVERLAYS` entries                                          |
| `name`         | `string`                     | Human-readable name for admin UIs                                                                    |
| `version`      | `string?`                    | Semver-ish; surfaced in `core.shell.manifest`                                                        |
| `surfaces`     | `('app' \| 'overlay')[]`     | Mount points the module presents to the shell                                                        |
| `description`  | `string?`                    | One-line description for admin UIs                                                                   |
| `dependsOn`    | `string[]?`                  | Other modules required at runtime; loader fails fast on missing deps                                 |
| `capabilities` | `Capability[]?`              | Typed RBAC capability identifiers (e.g. `finance.transaction.read`); supersedes free-form `provides` |
| `frontend`     | `ModuleFrontendManifest?`    | Routes, nav config, overlay mount slot                                                               |
| `backend`      | `ModuleBackendManifest?`     | tRPC router, MCP tool surface, ingest sources                                                        |
| `settings`     | `SettingsManifest?`          | Per-module settings sections (PRD-093 slot)                                                          |
| `features`     | `FeatureManifest[]?`         | Per-module feature toggles (PRD-094 slot)                                                            |
| `search`       | `SearchAdapterDescriptor[]?` | Query adapters and result render hints                                                               |
| `uriHandler`   | `UriHandlerDescriptor?`      | Object types this module owns under `pops:{id}/{type}/{...}` (ADR-012)                               |
| `aiTools`      | `AiToolDescriptor[]?`        | MCP / Ego-callable tools the module exposes                                                          |
| `migrations`   | `MigrationDescriptor[]?`     | SQL migration files this module owns; runner skips them when the module is not installed             |

`provides` is removed in favour of `capabilities` â€” the change is mechanical (free-form strings â†’ typed scope ids) and is the surface RBAC will consume when it lands.

The contract is the only API a module is required to export. The platform discovers everything else through the aggregated registry.

## Build-Time Registry

`@pops/module-registry` is a tiny generated package containing one file: a typed `MODULES` constant aggregating every installed manifest.

- Generated by a `pnpm registry:build` step that runs before `pnpm build` / `pnpm dev` (Turbo task with explicit `dependsOn`).
- Generator reads the env contract (`POPS_APPS`, `POPS_OVERLAYS`) plus a fixed `KNOWN_MODULES` list, imports each module's `manifest`, validates via `assertModuleManifest()`, and emits a typed `MODULES` array.
- Output is a real `.ts` file checked into `packages/module-registry/src/generated.ts` (committed; CI verifies it's up to date â€” same pattern as drizzle migrations).
- The `MODULES` constant is `as const` and statically inferable â€” consumers get the exact union of installed module ids in their types.
- Type-only consumers may also import `KNOWN_MODULES` (the full set of buildable modules, independent of install).

The shell, API root, search engine, settings page, features page, AI overlay, URI resolver, and migration runner all read from this single source.

## Consumers

Each existing hand-rolled registry collapses into a registry consumer. The shape stays the same; the source moves.

| Concern         | Today                                                       | After PRD-101                                                              |
| --------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Routing         | Shell hard-codes lazy imports; `RequireModule` guards       | Shell maps over `MODULES` filtering by `surfaces.includes('app')`          |
| API composition | `appRouter` hard-codes router list; `moduleGate` middleware | Root router composed from `MODULES.map(m => m.backend?.router)`            |
| Settings        | `settingsRegistry.register(...)` side-effect imports        | `MODULES.flatMap(m => m.settings)` â€” registry deleted                      |
| Features        | `featuresRegistry.register(...)` side-effect imports        | `MODULES.flatMap(m => m.features)` â€” registry deleted                      |
| Search          | Adapter files import `registerSearchAdapter` as side-effect | `MODULES.flatMap(m => m.search)` â€” `registerSearchAdapter` deleted         |
| Overlays        | `RootLayout` hard-codes `<ChatOverlay />`                   | Shell mounts overlays into `chromeSlot` declared in each manifest          |
| AI tools        | Cerebrum MCP tools registered ad-hoc per module             | Ego / MCP server reads `MODULES.flatMap(m => m.backend?.aiTools)`          |
| URI resolution  | No resolver                                                 | Resolver dispatches by `pops:{id}/{type}/...` to manifest-declared handler |
| Migrations      | Runner runs every file unconditionally                      | Runner runs only `MODULES.flatMap(m => m.backend?.migrations)`             |

`moduleGate` middleware stays as a defence-in-depth check on the procedure path; the static `appRouter` shape no longer depends on it.

## Edge Cases

| Case                                                                 | Behaviour                                                                                                         |
| -------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Module declares `dependsOn: ['finance']` but `POPS_APPS=media`       | Registry build fails at boot with a clear message naming the missing dep.                                         |
| Two modules declare the same `uriHandler` type prefix                | Registry build fails â€” collisions are a contract violation, not a runtime warning.                                |
| Module declares an overlay slot the shell doesn't define             | Shell logs a warning and skips mounting; module still functions in any other declared surface.                    |
| URI references an absent module: `pops:media/movie/42` with no media | Resolver returns a "module not installed" placeholder result, not an exception. Closes #2522.                     |
| Universal search includes adapters from absent modules               | Engine filters by `MODULES` before fan-out; no procedure call is attempted. Closes #2522.                         |
| Migration declared by absent module                                  | Runner skips; nothing recorded in `schema_migrations`. Re-enabling the module re-runs on next boot. Closes #2523. |
| Manifest validation fails at build                                   | `pnpm registry:build` exits non-zero with the offending module id and the failed assertion. CI catches.           |
| New module added without registering in `KNOWN_MODULES`              | Registry build fails â€” `KNOWN_MODULES` is the source of truth for buildable modules.                              |

## User Stories

| #   | Story                                                                     | Summary                                                                                                            | Parallelisable   |
| --- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------- |
| 01  | [us-01-extend-manifest](us-01-extend-manifest.md)                         | Extend `ModuleManifest` with `capabilities`, `features`, `search`, `uriHandler`, `aiTools`, `migrations` slots     | Yes              |
| 02  | [us-02-build-time-registry](us-02-build-time-registry.md)                 | `@pops/module-registry` package generated from manifests + env contract; CI guard                                  | Blocked by 01    |
| 03  | [us-03-routing-from-registry](us-03-routing-from-registry.md)             | Shell routing and API composition read from `MODULES`; remove hard-coded import lists                              | Blocked by 02    |
| 04  | [us-04-settings-from-registry](us-04-settings-from-registry.md)           | Settings page consumes `MODULES`; delete `settingsRegistry.register`                                               | Blocked by 02    |
| 05  | [us-05-features-from-registry](us-05-features-from-registry.md)           | Features admin consumes `MODULES`; delete `featuresRegistry.register`                                              | Blocked by 02    |
| 06  | [us-06-search-from-registry](us-06-search-from-registry.md)               | Search engine consumes `MODULES`; delete `registerSearchAdapter` side-effect imports. Closes #2522 (search half).  | Blocked by 02    |
| 07  | [us-07-overlay-mount-from-registry](us-07-overlay-mount-from-registry.md) | Shell mounts overlays into `chromeSlot` declared in manifest; remove hard-wired `<ChatOverlay />`                  | Blocked by 02    |
| 08  | [us-08-uri-resolver](us-08-uri-resolver.md)                               | Implement ADR-012 resolver as a registry consumer; absent-module URIs return placeholder. Closes #2522 (URI half). | Blocked by 02    |
| 09  | [us-09-migration-runner](us-09-migration-runner.md)                       | Runner consumes per-module migration declarations; absent modules skip. Closes #2523.                              | Blocked by 02    |
| 10  | [us-10-ai-tool-aggregation](us-10-ai-tool-aggregation.md)                 | Ego / MCP server enumerates tools from `MODULES`; per-module ad-hoc registration removed                           | Blocked by 02    |
| 11  | [us-11-test-matrix](us-11-test-matrix.md)                                 | Contract violations fail at build; install-set matrix exercised in CI                                              | Blocked by 03â€“10 |

## Out of Scope

- **RBAC enforcement.** `capabilities` is a contract slot ready for a future RBAC layer; no enforcement ships with this PRD.
- **Hot-register on env change.** Restart is required (same as PRD-100).
- **Tier 2 admin Modules page.** Install/remove from UI lands in a later PRD.
- **Cross-module composition (e.g. plug-in apps inside an app).** The contract is recursive in shape, but no second-level mounting is implemented.
- **Plexus adapter integration.** Cerebrum's PRD-090 plugin system is internal to cerebrum (external data-source ingestion) and out of scope for the shell-level contract. Cerebrum exposes `backend.aiTools` and `backend.router` like any module; what it does internally with Plexus is its own business.
